tldr: Not a drop-in swap: Elasticsearch is a lexical/full-text engine, Pinecone is a managed vector DB. You'll re-architect around embeddings, or run them side-by-side for hybrid search.
why_switch: Need semantic/vector search over embeddings instead of keyword/BM25
Want a fully managed service without cluster ops
Sub-50ms ANN queries at scale without tuning Lucene
Tighter fit for RAG and AI-native workloads
what_to_export: Source documents (raw text, IDs, metadata) via _source
Any precomputed embeddings stored in dense_vector fields
Filter/facet metadata used for hybrid queries
Synonym lists, analyzers, tokenizer configs (for reference, not portable)
Query logs and relevance tuning notes
Access roles and API key inventory
Snapshot of the cluster for rollback
export_method: Use Elasticsearch scroll API or _search_after to dump documents to NDJSON; elasticdump or a custom script works. Snapshot to S3-compatible storage as a backup.
import_method: Generate embeddings for each document with your chosen model (OpenAI, Cohere, open-source, etc.), then upsert vectors with metadata into a Pinecone index via the official SDK in batches.

concept_mapping.note: Pinecone index is per-dimension/metric; one Pinecone index ~= one ES index in role
Reuse the same string id to keep external references stable
Pinecone metadata is filter-only and size-limited; keep raw text in your own store
Embed the query string with the same model used at index time
No lexical analysis in Pinecone; handled at the embedding model level
No aggregations; do counts/facets in your app or a separate store

effort: 2-6 weeks for a single index; longer if you depend heavily on aggregations, custom analyzers, or BM25 relevance
gotchas: Pinecone has no full-text/BM25 — pure keyword queries will regress unless you keep ES or add a hybrid layer
Embedding cost and latency become a new line item at index and query time
Metadata size limits mean you cannot store full documents alongside vectors
Re-embedding everything is required if you change models
No aggregations or analytics — plan a separate store if you need them
Lock-in shifts from infra ops to a proprietary vector format and pricing model

email.subject: Migrating our search from Elasticsearch to Pinecone
email.body:
Hi team,

We're moving our search workload from Elasticsearch to Pinecone to support semantic/vector search and reduce cluster ops. This is not a like-for-like swap: Pinecone has no BM25 or aggregations, so we'll re-architect around embeddings and keep a lightweight metadata store for filters.

Plan:
1. Export documents from ES via scroll API.
2. Generate embeddings with our chosen model.
3. Upsert vectors + metadata into Pinecone in batches, reusing existing IDs.
4. Run both systems in parallel, shadow-test queries, then cut over.

Expected effort: 2-6 weeks. Main risks: relevance regressions on keyword-heavy queries and ongoing embedding costs. I'll share a more detailed design doc this week.

Thanks
